description: CephBucketTopic represents a Ceph Object Topic for Bucket Notifications

apiVersion.description:
APIVersion defines the versioned schema of this representation of an object.
Servers should convert recognized schemas to the latest internal value, and
may reject unrecognized values.
More info: https://git.k8s.io/community/contributors/devel/sig-architecture/api-conventions.md#resources

kind.description:
Kind is a string value representing the REST resource this object represents.
Servers may infer this from the endpoint the client submits requests to.
Cannot be updated.
In CamelCase.
More info: https://git.k8s.io/community/contributors/devel/sig-architecture/api-conventions.md#types-kinds

spec.description: BucketTopicSpec represent the spec of a Bucket Topic

endpoint.description: Contains the endpoint spec of the topic

ackLevel.description: The ack level required for this topic (none/broker/routeable)

disableVerifySSL.description: Indicate whether the server certificate is validated by the client or not

exchange.description: Name of the exchange that is used to route messages based on topics

uri.description: The URI of the AMQP endpoint to push notification to

disableVerifySSL.description: Indicate whether the server certificate is validated by the client or not

sendCloudEvents.description: Send the notifications with the CloudEvents header: https://github.com/cloudevents/spec/blob/main/cloudevents/adapters/aws-s3.md

uri.description: The URI of the HTTP endpoint to push notification to

ackLevel.description: The ack level required for this topic (none/broker)

disableVerifySSL.description: Indicate whether the server certificate is validated by the client or not

mechanism.description: The authentication mechanism for this topic (PLAIN/SCRAM-SHA-512/SCRAM-SHA-256/GSSAPI/OAUTHBEARER)

passwordSecretRef.description: The kafka password to use for authentication

key.description: The key of the secret to select from.  Must be a valid secret key.

name.description:
Name of the referent.
This field is effectively required, but due to backwards compatibility is
allowed to be empty. Instances of this type with an empty value here are
almost certainly wrong.
More info: https://kubernetes.io/docs/concepts/overview/working-with-objects/names/#names

optional.description: Specify whether the Secret or its key must be defined

uri.description: The URI of the Kafka endpoint to push notification to

useSSL.description: Indicate whether to use SSL when communicating with the broker

userSecretRef.description: The kafka user name to use for authentication

key.description: The key of the secret to select from.  Must be a valid secret key.

name.description:
Name of the referent.
This field is effectively required, but due to backwards compatibility is
allowed to be empty. Instances of this type with an empty value here are
almost certainly wrong.
More info: https://kubernetes.io/docs/concepts/overview/working-with-objects/names/#names

optional.description: Specify whether the Secret or its key must be defined

objectStoreName.description: The name of the object store on which to define the topic

objectStoreNamespace.description: The namespace of the object store on which to define the topic

opaqueData.description: Data which is sent in each event

persistent.description: Indication whether notifications to this endpoint are persistent or not

status.description: BucketTopicStatus represents the Status of a CephBucketTopic

ARN.description: The ARN of the topic generated by the RGW

observedGeneration.description: ObservedGeneration is the latest generation observed by the controller.

name.description: name is unique within a namespace to reference a secret resource.

namespace.description: namespace defines the space within which the secret name must be unique.

uid.description:
UID is a type that holds unique ID values, including UUIDs.  Because we
don't ONLY use UUIDs, this is an alias to string.  Being a type captures
intent and helps make sure that UIDs and names do not get conflated.
